title: Thelypteris mollis
taxonomy: Thelypteris mollis is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or the wood ferns. Thelypteris mollis belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen ferns. The species Thelypteris mollis is native to the Americas, specifically in the southeastern parts of the United States and Mexico. Thelypteris mollis is further classified as a species within the Thelypteris genus, which includes other species like Thelypteris kunthii and Thelypteris torresiana. The classification of Thelypteris mollis is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its frond shape and leaf structure.

intro: Thelypteris mollis, also known as the soft maiden fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The soft maiden fern is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and tannins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris mollis is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into salves and poultices for topical application.
